summary: 6 invented stories, one image each: The Window, The Table, The Bench, The Doorway, The Fork, The Lights.
style: painterly cinematic stills, one per story

scenes.title: The Window
scenes.quote: the freedom terrifies her more than the loss does, because freedom means she has to decide who she is now
scenes.image_prompt: Woman in silhouette at twilight window, hand touching glass, breath fog visible. Deep blue-violet dusk outside with amber streetlights just flickering on. Behind her, a dim apartment interior with shadowy furniture forms. Composition emphasizes the threshold moment between dark and light, inside and outside. Painterly brushwork, Edward Hopper-like isolation and color palette.

scenes.title: The Table
scenes.quote: I needed you here. I kept thinking you'd walk in.
scenes.image_prompt: Kitchen interior, late evening. Two siblings at opposite ends of a worn wooden table—man leaning forward with open palms, woman sitting back with crossed arms. Between them, an untouched folder and cold mug of tea. Overhead light creates stark shadows. Muted palette of browns and grays with single warm accent from distant stove light. Composition emphasizes the space between them. Painterly, Edward Hopper-like emotional isolation.

scenes.title: The Bench
scenes.quote: He's looking at his hands because his hands look like his father's now, and that frightens him too
scenes.image_prompt: A middle-aged man alone on a hospital waiting room bench, harsh fluorescent light from above casting unflattering shadows. He's hunched forward, elbows on knees, turning a small brass button between his fingers. Empty plastic chair beside him. Sterile mint-green walls, institutional clock visible but blurred. Paint in the style of Edward Hopper meets Lucian Freud - isolation and raw humanity. Muted palette: sickly fluorescent whites, hospital greens, the warm brass glint of the button catching light.

scenes.title: The Doorway
scenes.quote: she doesn't close the door behind her. She leaves it open, the dark room breathing quietly at her back, available.
scenes.image_prompt: Woman silhouetted in doorway between two rooms, one foot in each space. Left: warm lamplight spills across a crowded party, wine glasses catching amber glow, figures in soft focus. Right: deep blue darkness, coats on a bed, window reflecting night. She stands perfectly centered, hand on doorframe, phone glowing faintly in her other hand. Chiaroscuro lighting creates stark division. Painterly, impressionistic style with rich ochres and midnight blues.

scenes.title: The Fork
scenes.quote: They reach the fork. They stop. And in the stopping, the not-mentioning becomes impossible
scenes.image_prompt: Two figures stand at a gravel path's fork in golden late afternoon light, surrounded by autumn trees. Sam and Athif face the diverging paths—one climbing toward a ridge, the other descending toward unseen water. Rendered in warm, impressionistic brushstrokes with amber and ochre tones. The composition centers on their silhouettes from behind, shoulders almost touching, caught in a moment of stillness before decision. Soft shadows stretch long across the gravel.

scenes.title: The Lights
scenes.quote: Not am I alone but I am here, and there is something out there, and the space between us is full of air I'm still breathing.
scenes.image_prompt: A solitary figure sits on a grassy hillside at deep blue hour, knees drawn up, face tilted toward distant amber lights scattered like fallen stars across the horizon. The lights blur between town and constellation, warm against indigo sky. The figure is small in the frame, rendered in loose brushstrokes, their posture open and still. Pre-dawn grey touches one edge of the canvas. Atmospheric, contemplative, painted in the style of late Turner meets Rothko's color fields.
